steps: The line of best fit passes through approximately the origin (0, 0), so the y-intercept is 0. This eliminates C and D, which are non-zero horizontal lines.
The line slopes upward from left to right (positive slope), eliminating B (y = -1.1x, negative slope).
Estimating slope from the line: it rises about 1.1 units for every 1 unit run, giving slope \approx 1.1.
Equation: y = 1.1x, which matches choice A.
explanation:
• Choice A — '$y = 1.1x$': Positive slope of 1.1 through the origin. Consistent with a line that rises steadily and passes near (0, 0) in a scatterplot with positively correlated data. ✓
  • Choice B — '$y = -1.1x$': Negative slope means the line falls from left to right. The scatterplot shows an upward-trending line, so this is the wrong direction. ✗
  • Choice C — '$y = 5.2$': A horizontal line at y = 5.2 indicates no relationship between x and y. A line of best fit through clearly trending data would not be horizontal. ✗
  • Choice D — '$y = -5.2$': A horizontal line at y = -5.2 below the x-axis. Neither horizontal nor at a reasonable y-value for the data shown. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
